title: Executive Order 7651—Modifying Executive Order of June 8, 1926, Creating Reservoir Site Reserve No. 17, Pacific Slope Basins, California

flags.justification: Authorizes Federal Power Commission licensing on specific lands, bounded by a protective condition preserving the reserve's primary purpose.
A specific, well-designed condition requires licensees to remove infrastructure at their own expense within thirty days of notice if the land is needed for reservoir purposes.
Single purpose despite extensive technical land descriptions.
The removal condition is precise: thirty days, at the licensee's own expense.

The removal-on-notice condition specifically prevents licensees from relying on their infrastructure remaining if the reservoir's primary purpose materializes, rather than creating an incentive problem.
A standing regulatory modification enabling an ongoing licensing practice with a built-in protective condition, not a temporary-authority grant.
The removal condition carefully preserves the reserve's primary purpose rather than removing any oversight.
No distinct third-party monitoring gap beyond what is already addressed by the removal condition.
A routine, technical land-use modification for a specific site.
Interior retains notice-and-reclaim rights over FPC-licensed activities, a well-coordinated design.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/16 = 0.0%. A genuinely well-designed, protective land-use modification balancing power-project licensing against the reserve's primary purpose.
